thesis: Leading cloud-native cybersecurity platform combining durable 25% revenue growth with expanding profitability and a widening competitive moat. The Falcon platform's AI-driven threat detection, 28+ module portfolio, and enterprise vendor consolidation tailwind position CrowdStrike as the primary beneficiary of security spend consolidation. Q1 FY2027 EPS beat (+25% vs est), FY27 ARR guidance raised to $6.532B–$6.556B (24–25% growth), and 30%+ FCF margins support a premium valuation. 4-for-1 stock split effective July 2, 2026. Medium-High conviction with 6-month base target $700–$750.

drivers: AI security TAM expansion via Charlotte AI and AI-native threat detection
Platform consolidation accelerates: module attach rates rise to 8–10 per customer
AWS partnership deepening drives cloud security ARR growth above 40% annually
Identity protection breakout becomes $1B+ ARR product competing with Okta

risks: Microsoft Defender bundling compresses mid-market pricing and slows new logo growth
Macro IT spending slowdown delays enterprise security upgrades
Multiple compression to 15x EV/ARR as growth decelerates from 25% to 15%
Platform complexity from 28+ modules degrades customer experience or increases churn

base_case.assumptions: Revenue grows 24–25% through FY2027, FCF margin sustains 30%+, market assigns 25x EV/ARR on best-in-class SaaS metrics and platform leadership

catalysts.event: Q2 FY2027 Earnings — confirms FY27 ARR guidance trajectory
Fal.Con annual user conference — new product announcements
Q3 FY2027 Earnings — first FY2028 guidance
Charlotte AI general availability and monetization metrics

key_risks: Premium valuation (25x EV/ARR) leaves limited room for execution missteps
Microsoft Defender bundling threat in price-sensitive mid-market segments
Natural growth deceleration at $4.5B+ scale could compress multiples
Product complexity risk from rapid expansion into cloud, identity, and observability
